items.title: Mara targets premium salaries for TVET graduates through industry partnerships
items.summary: "Many Mara programmes involve industry collaborations to ensure our TVET graduates maintain an employability rate of above 99 per cent. The programme combines theoretical learning, practical training, industry exposure and professional certification from Schneider Electric Industries (M) Sdn Bhd. To support the initiative, Mara signed MoUs with Bumi Perkasa Engineering Sdn Bhd, Starlite Electrical Industries Sdn Bhd and Advance Industrial Solutions Sdn Bhd.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 20, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's benchmark index closed in the red on Monday dragged mainly by losses in the financial services sector. The key index dropped nearly 10 points to 1,722 points. UpCnGwjjgus © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Impact Capital partners China's KeenData to advance AI software in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Impact Capital Holdings Bhd's unit, Impact Business Solutions Sdn Bhd, has signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Beijing Kejie Technology Co Ltd (KeenData) to promote data and artificial intelligence (AI) foundational software in Malaysia. Impact Capital non-independent executive director Chyr Ye Hong said the partnership would place significant emphasis on talent development through training programmes, staff exchanges, professional development initiatives and certification programmes. KeenData is recognised for its expertise in AI-native data infrastructure and intelligent analytics solutions.

items.title: AliExpress hit with US$629 million EU fine over sales of illegal, counterfeit products
items.summary: BRUSSELS: Alibaba's AliExpress was hit with a record €550 million (US$629 million) fine from the European Union (EU) on Monday for failing to tackle sales of illegal, unsafe and counterfeit products on its platform. The Commission charged AliExpress in June last year with failing to comply with a DSA requirement to assess and mitigate the risks of dissemination of illegal products. She pointed to AliExpress's 193 million users in Europe last year versus Shein's 156 million and Temu's 130 million.

items.title: EC issues 13,263 postal ballots for Negri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The Election Commission (EC) has issued 13,263 postal ballot papers for the 16th Negri Sembilan election. EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said 12,699 of the postal ballots were issued under Category 1A, covering election officials, EC members and officers, police and armed forces personnel, as well as media practitioners. A total of 343 postal ballots were issued under Category 1B for Malaysian citizens residing overseas, while 251 were issued under Category 1C for agencies and organisations in the safety and health sectors.

items.title: Ringgit ends higher against US dollar on robust economic data
items.summary: Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said Malaysia's data points continue to suggest that its economic fundamentals remain robust, with total exports in June jumping 45.4 per cent year-on-year, the third consecutive month of double-digit expansion. This could mean the current account surplus balance could go higher in the April-June quarter, which would provide fundamental support to the ringgit," he told Bernama. Malaysia's exports expanded 45.4 per cent to RM177.9 billion and imports rose 43.9 per cent to RM163.0 billion, with the trade surplus surging by 64.9 per cent to RM14.9 billion in the month reviewed.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia closes lower on profit taking in banking stocks
items.summary: Among heavyweight counters, Malayan Banking Bhd decreased 18 sen to RM11.02, Public Bank Bhd fell six sen to RM5.19, and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd shed seven sen to RM7.75, while Tenaga Nasional Bhd and IHH Healthcare Bhd were flat at RM14.30 and RM8.50, respectively. As for the active stocks, Inari Amertron Bhd rose by 11 sen to RM2.28, while Pegasus Heights Bhd and Aimax Bhd were flat at half-a-sen and one sen, respectively, Zetrix AI Bhd eased 2.5 sen to 72 sen, and Tanco Holdings Bhd gave up three sen to 30.5 sen. Of the top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd garnered RM1.46 sen to RM44.66, Vitrox Corp Bhd jumped 41 sen to RM7.69, Westports Holdings Bhd surged 38 sen to RM6.53, Panasonic Manufacturing Malaysia Bhd increased by 34 sen to RM5.64, and Kelington Group Bhd added 28 sen to RM7.78. For the top losers, Nestle (M) Bhd shrank 78 sen to RM92.66, Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd erased 48 sen to RM18.34, Hong Leong Bank Bhd retreated 20 sen to RM22.24, and RHB Bank Bhd slid 16 sen to RM8.37.

items.title: Seven states brace for thunderstorms, heavy rain until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in several areas across seven states until 9pm today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said the affected areas included Sik and Baling in Kedah, and Hulu Perak and Batang Padang in Perak. Similar conditions are expected in parts of Terengganu, including Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu and Marang, as well as several districts in Pahang, including Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Jerantut, Maran, Kuantan, Pekan and Rompin.

items.title: Man caught making multiple petrol runs for Thailand smuggling
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: A 36-year-old man was arrested at a petrol station here yesterday after he was allegedly caught repeatedly buying petrol for smuggling into Thailand. General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the suspect was detained during Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan/Op Tiris about 10am following intelligence on petrol smuggling activities. "The suspect first filled up 89.63 litres of petrol about 9.17am.

items.title: MACC, PNB strengthen strategic ties to bolster integrity in national investment governance
items.summary: MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said integrity-driven governance was fundamental to maintaining investor confidence in funds managed by PNB. "PNB must continue to uphold integrity and strengthen investors' trust in the management of unit trust investments in the country," he said after receiving a courtesy call from PNB president and group chief executive Datuk Rizal Rickman Ramli at the MACC headquarters today. Also present were MACC deputy chief commissioner (Prevention) Datuk Azmi Kamaruzaman and PNB board member Tan Sri Datuk Zainun Ali.

items.title: Sunway-led JV wins S$2.13bil Singapore land tender
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Bhd's joint ventures, Gemini Residential Pte Ltd and Gemini Trustee Pte Ltd, have been awarded a parcel of land at Bayshore Drive, Singapore, for S$2.13 billion (approximately RM6.74 billion) by the Urban Redevelopment Authority of Singapore. Gemini Residential Pte Ltd and Gemini Trustee Pte Ltd are 30 per cent-owned joint ventures of Sunway, a conglomerate with businesses spanning property, construction, hospitality, retail, leisure, education, trading and manufacturing, quarrying, building materials and real estate investment trusts. "The proposed project is subject to usual risks associated with property development and construction, including fluctuations in raw material prices, interest rate movements and gyrations in the property cycle.

items.title: Umno denies joint BN-PN manifesto for Negri Sembilan polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has denied that Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) are working on a joint manifesto for the Negri Sembilan election. Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the understanding between the two parties was limited to avoiding seat clashes and did not involve any formal cooperation. His remarks came after PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said the newly established PN special working committee would finalise recommendations and input for a joint manifesto with BN for the Negri Sembilan election.

items.title: AGC orders court inquiry into Durian Tunggal police shooting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Attorney General's Chambers (AGC) has ordered a formal inquest into the deaths of three men fatally shot during a police operation in Durian Tunggal, Melaka, on November 24, 2025. The decision follows a comprehensive review of investigation papers submitted by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). “The Attorney General's Chambers takes seriously every incident involving the loss of life.

items.title: Velesto cuts short Naga 8 contract after completing 3 out of 12 wells
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Velesto Energy Bhd has terminated its jack-up drilling rig services contract for Naga 8 without cause. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said Velesto Drilling Sdn Bhd has completed Phase 1 of the project, comprising three of the 12 commitment wells over about eight months. The company said its clients namely PC Ketapang II Ltd, PC North Madura II Ltd and Petronas North Ketapang Sdn Bhd together with Velesto Energy's board agreed to terminate the contract on July 7.

items.title: Umno maintains commitment to unity government until next election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has reiterated that it will not make a "tebuk atap" (backdoor) move to topple the unity government before the end of its term, its secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said. Asyraf said Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi had repeatedly maintained that the party would not resort to such a move, as the unity government was formed based on an agreement among political coalitions. "However, we should not mix up state elections with the understanding and agreement that exists at the federal level," he said.

items.title: Reservoir Link secures gas shipping, LNG import licences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Reservoir Link Energy Bhd's unit Reservoir Link Sdn Bhd has received the Energy Commission's approval for a gas shipping licence and an import into regasification terminal licence under the Gas Supply Act. Reservoir Link executive deputy chairman Thien Chiet Chai said the approvals marked a significant milestone in the group's expansion into Malaysia's regulated natural gas sector. The gas shipping licence allows Reservoir Link to transport natural gas through Malaysia's gas transportation system, while the import into regasification terminal licence enables the company to import LNG into licensed regasification terminals.

items.title: Mi Technovation Q2 profit more than doubles on semiconductor materials
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mi Technovation Bhd's net profit more than doubled to RM55.21 million in the second quarter ended June 30, 2026 from RM15.78 million a year ago lifted by its semiconductor material business. Revenue rose 46.7 per cent to RM226.02 million from RM154.12 million, driven by a 6.2 per cent increase in the semiconductor equipment segment to RM93.9 million, while sales of semiconductor materials more than doubled to RM131.3 million. For the cumulative six months ended June 30, 2026, the company's net profit jumped to RM86.19 million from RM33.6 million a year earlier, while revenue rose to RM393.78 million from RM273.67 million.

items.title: Mita withdraws tour bus fare announcement after MyCC probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (Mita) has withdrawn its ceiling fare announcement for tour buses and vans after acknowledging that the move could raise competition concerns under the Competition Act 2010. "All tour bus and tour van operators remain free to independently determine and set their own prices in accordance with their respective commercial considerations," it said. Mita said it had voluntarily provided an undertaking to MyCC under Section 43 of the Competition Act 2010, committing to avoid anti-competitive conduct and ensure compliance with the law among its office bearers and members.

items.title: eFishery setback won't affect pension payments, says Tengku Zafrul [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The recent investment loss involving Indonesian aquaculture firm eFishery has not significantly affected the financial position of the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) or its ability to fund civil service pensions, says Senior Political Adviser to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz. "The entire eFishery investment accounted for less than 1 per cent of KWAP's investment income in 2024 and less than 0.1 per cent of the total fund size in 2025," he said. Tengku Zafrul said KWAP's total fund size stood at RM195 billion in 2025, supported by RM18 billion in investment income generated in 2024.

items.title: Education, Health ministries urged to tackle rising Type 2 diabetes among teens
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The People's Progressive Party's (PPP) Youth wing has urged the Education and Health ministries to step up collaboration to curb the rising cases of Type 2 diabetes among teenagers. Its deputy Youth chief Janarthanan Velliathum said the trend, highlighted by the health minister's recent statement that Type 2 diabetes had been detected in children as young as 14, underscored a growing public health challenge that demanded urgent action. "The health of the younger generation is a national asset.

items.title: Defence and minerals anchor Pakistan's lobbying push in Washington
items.summary: AS Pakistan stepped into the global centre-stage through Iran war mediation, it hired a US lobbying firm to push Washington for boosted security cooperation and minerals investment, the contract shows. The two-year, US$1.2-million lobbying agreement, filed in a public official database, began in May, spanning a period officials and experts say is critical to converting the goodwill Pakistan has earned with Donald Trump's administration into tangible bilateral benefits. Pakistan's latest agreement, disclosed under the US Foreign Agents Registration Act, provides direct insight into Islamabad's strategic priorities.

items.title: Asyraf: Focus on policies, not personal attacks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has never used personal attacks as a political strategy in the Johor and Negri Sembilan election campaigns, instead choosing to focus on policies and its track record. Its secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said neither Umno nor Barisan Nasional (BN) leaders had attacked the unity government at the federal level, adding that the coalition was focused on presenting its achievements and plans for the states. "We respect our partners in the unity government.

items.title: A-GC orders inquest into deaths of trio in Durian Tunggal shooting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Attorney General's Chambers (A-GC) has directed that an inquest be held into the deaths of three men who were killed in a shooting incident in Durian Tunggal, Melaka, in November last year. "The purpose of the inquest is to determine the cause and circumstances of the deaths and to examine the facts and circumstances leading to the incident, including whether any criminal elements were involved in the deaths of the three persons," the statement said. The three victims — T. Pooneswaran, 24, M. Puspanathan, 21 and M. Logeswaran, 29 — were killed during a police operation in Durian Tunggal on Nov 24 last year.

items.title: Van service helps disabled, elderly patients get to appointments
items.summary: KUANTAN: The challenges faced by families taking disabled or chronically ill members for hospital appointments often go unnoticed, despite becoming increasingly common. Recognising the financial and emotional strain on caregivers, Semambu assemblyman Chan Chun Kuang introduced Jom Teman Hospital, an initiative providing reliable transport to healthcare facilities to ensure patients do not miss appointments. For patients without anyone to accompany them, Chan said volunteers could be arranged to assist them throughout their hospital visits at RM11 an hour.

items.title: Premier hails AirBorneo’s RM374.88 fare, warns: raise it and you’ll face trouble
items.summary: KUCHING, July 20 — The ticket prices offered by AirBorneo Airways (AirBorneo) serve as a benchmark for other airlines, helping to stabilise airfares in the market, said Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. He said when other airlines saw the fares offered by the state-owned aviation company, they would be compelled to lower their own ticket prices in order to remain competitive. “We are at the mercy of these other airlines because they do not have Sarawak at heart,” he said when launching AirBorneo’s inaugural Kuching (KCH)–Kuala Lumpur (KUL) flight at Kuching International Airport today.

items.title: Floods, landslides across northern India kill at least 25
items.summary: SRINAGAR: At least 25 people died in floods and landslides caused by torrential rain across northern and northeastern India over the weekend, officials said on Monday, with more rain predicted over the next few days. At least 15 people were killed in separate rain-related incidents in Poonch, Rajouri and Doda districts of Indian-administered Kashmir, while several others were reported missing, a senior police official said. In the neighbouring state of Assam, one person died on Sunday in floods, the state disaster management body said.

items.title: World Cup litter at Dataran Merdeka draws criticism online
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The litter left behind at Dataran Merdeka after thousands gathered to watch the Fifa World Cup final has sparked widespread debate online. Instagram user @cc010170 described the behaviour as "uncivilised", while @Angelabriones1411 called it "a shameful, disgraceful culture mindset." Facebook user @Celine Dcruz said: "Don't blame the crowd.

items.title: Delivery rider charged with sexually assaulting stepdaughter, threatening cops
items.summary: MELAKA: A 32-year-old food delivery rider was charged in two separate courts here today with sexually assaulting his stepdaughter and threatening two policemen and a family member. The accused pleaded not guilty to all charges before the Sessions Court and Magistrate's Court. At the Sessions Court, he was charged with sexually assaulting his 13-year-old stepdaughter at an apartment in Bukit Baru on the evening of July 13.

items.title: Danube water levels reach lowest in 30 years, hampering farming and shipping
items.summary: BUCHAREST: Water levels of the Danube river in Romania fell to their lowest since 1996 over the weekend, prompting irrigation restrictions for farmers and partially disrupting shipping and tourism along its banks. Romania's state water-management agency said the level at the Romanian entry point of Europe's second-longest river was 1,700 cubic metres of water per second on Sunday, against a July average range of 4,700 cubic metres per second. Authorities had restricted farmer access to water for crop irrigation in southeastern Romania and controlled water reservoirs to ensure minimum levels required for cooling state nuclear power producer Nuclearelectrica's two reactors.

items.title: AirBorneo's state-backed model aims to keep fares affordable, says Abang Johari
items.summary: SEPANG: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd (AirBorneo) business model will differ from that of conventional commercial airlines with the Sarawak government contributing towards fixed costs as part of its strategy to improve connectivity and support the state's economic development. Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said the state viewed AirBorneo as an "air bridge" rather than solely as a commercial airline, reflecting Sarawak's geographical separation from Peninsular Malaysia. "We have built more than 20 bridges in Sarawak over the last four years, and AirBorneo will extend that connectivity through an air bridge."

items.title: Aminuddin urges integrity in Negeri Sembilan polls, condemns vandalism of campaign materials
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 20 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has called on all political parties to conduct their campaigns with integrity and in full compliance with election laws and regulations throughout the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election. He was commenting on a statement issued earlier today by the PH Media Centre Secretariat for the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, which said the coalition’s main operations centre had received several reports of vandalism involving campaign materials on the second day of the campaign. For the latest updates on the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9.

items.title: Zahid warns of fraud surge, says Cyber Crime Bill 2026 will fortify Malaysia’s defences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A total of 8,014 charges related to online fraud crimes have been recorded as of May this year, surpassing the total of 6,140 charges throughout 2025, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said the statistics showed that the threat of online fraud crimes was becoming more serious not only in terms of the increasing number of cases, but also the increasing financial losses to the people. Ahmad Zahid said the arrest statistics showed the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM)’s continued action in combating online fraud crimes.

items.title: Sabah seeks RM1.6bil in supplementary supply bill
items.summary: State Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said the extra allocation is necessary to cover six main expenditure categories, led by RM856 million for statutory funds. The remaining allocation comprises RM278 million for operating expenditure, RM210 million for investment expenditure, RM162 million for administrative expenditure, RM93 million for domestic grants, and RM13 million for special allocations. Under the ministry's breakdown, the Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Ministry will receive RM43 million, the Rural Development Ministry RM30.6 million, and the Youth Development, Sports and Creative Economy Ministry RM24 million.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: PSM candidate for Rahang learns lesson from 'sosah' slogan controversy
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) candidate for Rahang said he never expected his campaign slogan to go viral after the word "sosah" was interpreted as having a different meaning. S. Tinagaran, 49, said the slogan "Lomah dibelo, sosah dibantu" ("The weak are defended, those in hardship are helped") was chosen after discussions with several parties, including local residents. Tinagaran, better known as Cikgu Tina, said his team had since replaced the social media poster with one using the standard Malay word "susah".

items.title: Trailer crash in Sik kills van driver, injures two including former Kedah assemblyman’s wife
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A man was killed while two others were injured in a three-vehicle crash involving a trailer lorry at Batu 34, Jalan Kuala Sin, Sik, Kedah, today. “Upon arrival at the scene, we found a road accident involving a container trailer lorry, a Nissan X-Trail SUV and a Toyota Hiace van,” he said in a statement today. The trailer lorry driver was not injured,” he said.

items.title: Sabah viral school assault: Six students charged with rioting, claim trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Six female students aged between 12 and 16 were charged in the Court for Children in Kota Kinabalu today with rioting over an alleged assault on a 13-year-old student outside a secondary school that was captured in a video which later went viral. The New Straits Times previously reported that the incident drew public attention after a video showing a group of students allegedly assaulting a Form One student circulated widely on social media on May 27. The six are represented by Datuk Ram Singh and Prabjit Singh Gill, while Deputy Public Prosecutor Arfa Anchal Khan Muhammad Munir Khan appeared for the prosecution.

items.title: Oil palm lorry driver killed after being pinned in cab in Jasin crash
items.summary: Berita Harian reported that the victim was pronounced dead at the scene by Health Ministry (MOH) personnel after he was found trapped in the driver’s cabin of the lorry. Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Public Relations Officer Mohd Hafidzatullah Rashid said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 11.50am. He said six firefighters from the Merlimau Fire and Rescue Station were dispatched to the scene with a Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) vehicle and an Emergency Medical Rescue Service (EMRS) unit.

items.title: Pet project in Parliament: Government weighs fund and tax breaks for animal welfare
items.summary: Political secretary at the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) Datuk Azman Abidin said that the lack of a special budget has forced most NGOs and animal shelters to operate voluntarily, using their own funds to manage and rescue stray animals. It requires the involvement of all parties, including government agencies, local authorities, NGOs, veterinarians, industry players, academia, and the community,” he said. Also present at the dialogue session were the Malaysian Animal Association deputy director-general Dr Aida Muhid, Malaysian Animal Association president Dr Arie Dwi Andika, and Department of Environmental Health director Dr Nor Halizam binti Ismail.

items.title: Prison walls get digital armour: Drone smuggling bid triggers zero‑tolerance tech blitz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Prison Department will enhance security measures, including using technology, intelligence monitoring and patrols, to counter the growing threat posed by drones being used for criminal activities. The proactive measures follow the department’s detection and successful prevention of an attempt to smuggle contraband into the Sungai Udang Prison Complex using a drone on July 15. “Through monitoring and security control measures by the prison, the suspicious activity was detected, and a police report was lodged, enabling enforcement action to be taken.

items.title: Johor cops nab 12 men, including teen, after brawl breaks out during World Cup final screening in JB
items.summary: Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said authorities received information regarding the incident at 5.40am. He said police also detected a viral video clip of the alleged incident that was uploaded on a Facebook group. On the arrests, Ab Rahaman said three of the suspects had records for previous criminal cases.

items.title: Man killed in suspected hit-and-run in Seremban while putting up party flags in N. Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A man was killed in a suspected hit-and-run incident while putting up party flags along Jalan Kuala Sawah-Rantau near Kampung Belangkan in Seremban on Sunday evening. The incident, which occurred at about 6.40pm, involved a lorry and the 23-year-old victim, according to a report by Sinar Harian . Seremban district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Yatim Osman said preliminary investigations found that the victim was on the left shoulder of the road heading towards Rantau while carrying out work to install party flags.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh blasts litterbugs, praises DBKL for extra‑time clean‑up
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh has expressed appreciation to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) workers who worked tirelessly to clean up Dataran Merdeka after the area was littered with rubbish following the public screening of the 2026 Fifa World Cup final. Hannah also urged the public to play their part in keeping public spaces clean, saying disposing of rubbish in bins is a basic civic responsibility that should not be left entirely to public workers. “Yes, I know every taxpayer deserves good public services.

items.title: Public nabs man after attempted house break-in in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 20 — A man’s attempt to trespass into a house in Kampung Melayu Majidee here was foiled after members of the public detained him before handing him over to police this morning. Johor Bahru South district police chief Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei said police received a report at 8.42am from a 33-year-old local man, who is also a police officer. Following the report, officers from the Majidee police station went to the scene and arrested the suspect at about 9am.

items.title: Kelantan police station chief remanded seven days as MACC probes alleged RM500 bribe
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 20 — A police station chief has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations into allegations that he accepted a RM500 bribe in exchange for not taking action against a foreign national. The 41-year-old suspect, who holds the rank of sergeant major, arrived at the Kota Bharu Court Complex at about 9.05am wearing the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) lock-up attire and was escorted by two MACC officers. Kelantan MACC director Azmin Yusoff confirmed that the suspect was arrested at about 2.20pm yesterday.

items.title: Kelantan labourer fined RM3,000 after toddler found with meth, benzodiazepines in system
items.summary: PASIR MAS, July 20 — A labourer was fined RM3,000, placed on a good behaviour bond and ordered to perform 36 hours of community service after pleading guilty to exposing his one-year-eight-month-old daughter to dangerous drugs two months ago. Muhammad Shahrul Hisham Lailatulazhma, 27, entered the guilty plea before Sessions Court Judge Zulkpli Abdullah, Kosmo! The accused paid both the fine and the good behaviour bond ordered by the court.

items.title: Sarawak’s jet age begins as Abang Johari joins AirBorneo maiden lift‑off to Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUCHING, July 20 — AirBorneo today made history with the commencement of its first jet operations through the inaugural Kuching-Kuala Lumpur flight. Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg was among the passengers on the maiden flight, MY5005, which departed from Kuching International Airport at 1.20pm for Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA). Like Sarawak, Japan is an island so connectivity is extremely important.

items.title: Sessions Court hears RM8m FashionValet transfer, Vivy Yusof and husband deny breach of trust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Sessions Court here was told today that RM8 million was transferred from FashionValet Sdn Bhd’s bank account to 30 Maple Sdn Bhd through two online transactions in August 2018. CIMB Bank’s Taman Tun Dr Ismail branch Customer Service Manager Azizan Hassan, 45, testified that a review of 30 Maple’s bank statements revealed online deposits of RM5 million and RM3 million from FashionValet’s bank account. “On August 21, 2018, there was a deposit of RM5 million from FashionValet Sdn Bhd with the transaction description, ‘REMITTANCE CR/ROC/PB ECP PAYMENT FASHIONVALET SDN BHD PB ECP PAYMENT FASHION VALET SDN BHD ADVANCES TO 30 MAPLE’.

items.title: Sabah pockets RM600m, presses Putrajaya for RM900m balance under interim deal
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 20 — Sabah has received RM600 million of the RM1.5 billion interim special grant from the federal government, with the balance of RM900 million requested to be paid within the year, said state Assistant Finance Minister Datuk Mohd Ishak Ayub. He said the RM1.5 billion payment announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is an interim arrangement and does not represent the final amount due to Sabah under the Federal Constitution. Other allocations include RM278 million for operational expenditure, RM210 million for investments, RM162 million for administrative expenses, RM93 million for domestic grants and RM13 million under special provisions.

items.title: Lab tests confirm Selangor ICU patient free of leptospirosis, health dept dismisses viral claims
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 20 — Laboratory tests have confirmed that a patient admitted to the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) of a hospital in Selangor on July 7 with suspected leptospirosis did not contract the disease, the Selangor State Health Department (JKNS) said today. “Of the total, 26 cases, or 21 per cent, were linked to recreational water activities,” said the statement, which was issued following the circulation of social media messages claiming a leptospirosis case had originated from a recreational water area in Selangor. It said three recreational water sites in Selangor frequently associated with leptospirosis cases are Taman Eko Rimba Sungai Kanching in Gombak, Sungai Congkak Waterfall in Hulu Langat and Sungai Ulu Yam in Hulu Selangor.

items.title: Malaysia welcomes Zhouzhuang partnership, pledges stronger cultural ties and heritage conservation with China
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Malaysia today welcomed a tourism and heritage cooperation initiative with China’s century-old Zhouzhuang Ancient Town, aimed at strengthening cultural exchange, sustainable tourism and heritage conservation between both countries. Ye Xin, general manager of Kunshan Water Town Zhouzhuang Cultural Tourism Development Group Co. Limited, said China and Malaysia share a common cultural heritage and a long history of cultural exchanges. “Malaysia’s thriving tourism industry and abundant outbound travel market make it a key overseas market for Zhouzhuang.

items.title: Immigration officer pleads not guilty to seven bribery charges totalling RM1,950
items.summary: MELAKA, July 20 — A Melaka Immigration Department officer pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court here today to seven charges of accepting bribes totalling RM1,950 last year. Mohd Nazrul Md Yasin, 39, entered the plea before Judge Mohamad Kamil Nizam. The foreign national, identified as Kusmiyati, was allegedly staying at the premises, an offence under the Immigration Act 1959/63.

items.title: Sweeping storm alert issued, MetMalaysia says treacherous weather threatens peninsula and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit several states in Peninsular Malaysia and Sarawak until 5pm today. MetMalaysia announced that the affected areas are Kuala Lumpur, Perlis, Penang, Melaka as well as in Kedah involving the areas of Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Kulim and Bandar Baharu as well as Perak in Manjung, Central Perak, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak and Muallim. Other states also affected are Negeri Sembilan in Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin as well as Johor in Tangkak, Muar, Batu Pahat and Johor Bahru.

items.title: MCMC regulates platform risks, not algorithms directly, Teo Nie Ching tells Dewan Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) does not directly regulate or determine the design of platform algorithms, instead adopting a risk-based regulatory approach that places responsibility on licensed service providers to manage their own systems. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the approach requires licensed service providers to assess and manage the impact of their systems, including taking reasonable measures to reduce the algorithmic amplification of unlawful or harmful content. “MCMC adopts a risk-based regulatory approach that places the responsibility on licensed service providers to assess and manage the impact of their systems.

items.title: Mechanic jailed 20 years, caned for raping, robbing teen jogger in Tumpat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A mechanic who assaulted, raped and robbed a 15-year-old girl while she was out jogging in Tumpat last month was sentenced to 20 years’ jail and seven strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here today. For the first charge, he was accused of raping the 15-year-old girl after causing injuries to her. For the second charge, Muhammad Faryzuan was accused of robbing the victim of her Vivo Y19 mobile phone while armed with a deadly weapon — a wooden stick.

items.title: Woman’s body found with hands, legs tied in Kota Tinggi river
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A woman’s body was found floating in Sungai Tanjung Serindit, Ulu Papan, Pengerang, Kota Tinggi last night, shocking residents after the victim was discovered with both her hands and legs tied with rope. The unidentified woman’s body was found after police received information from members of the public at about 9pm, Kosmo! Kota Tinggi district police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said a team from the Kota Tinggi district police headquarters and the Johor police contingent’s forensic unit rushed to the scene after receiving the report.

items.title: Economy Ministry: Govt readying measures as Middle East conflict pushes up oil prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The government is prepared to take the appropriate measures to safeguard the people’s welfare and sustain the country’s economic growth following the renewed escalation of geopolitical conflict in West Asia, said Deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah. Hence, Mohd Shahar said, the government is closely monitoring the situation as it has the potential to put pressure on industrial production costs and the prices of goods, particularly if the conflict persists. “The government has mobilised various strategic and integrated intervention measures based on four key approaches: ensuring supplies, prolonging supply availability, curbing price increases, and capitalising on available opportunities.

items.title: PwC tapped to chart Penang’s roadmap for international financial hub, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 20 — The Penang state government has appointed PricewaterhouseCoopers Advisory Services Sdn Bhd (PwC) as the consultant to prepare the white paper, framework and action plan for the proposed Penang International Financial Centre (PIFC). Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said this decision was made by the PIFC special task force committee. He said PwC was given 20 weeks to complete the white paper, strategic framework (blueprint) and action plan as well as set the direction for the establishment of the PIFC.

items.title: Sungai Bakap Tamil school project gets 2027 deadline after long delays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The construction project for Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan (Tamil) (SJKT) Sungai Bakap, Penang, which has been delayed since 2015, is expected to be fully completed in the fourth quarter of 2027. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the project was continued after the Prime Minister approved a ceiling cost of RM12,667,367.59 with Yayasan Didik Negara appointed as the implementing agency. However, the Sessions Court dismissed the case filed against the MOE in May 2026,” she said.

items.title: ‘Who will pay for it?’ Anwar defends Malaysia’s fuel policy, warns against piling on debt
items.summary: KAJANG, July 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has defended the government’s fuel pricing policy, saying Malaysia’s ability to keep petrol prices at RM1.90 per litre comes despite the country still importing nearly half of petroleum requirements. Responding to criticism saying he had failed to reduce fuel prices, Anwar said the government had to consider the long-term financial burden of subsidies and existing debts inherited from previous administrations. Anwar said while some may call for further reductions in fuel prices, the government must consider how such measures would be financed.

items.title: Two arrested after drone smuggling attempt foiled at Melaka’s Sungai Udang Prison
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Prisons Department has foiled an attempt to smuggle prohibited items into the Sungai Udang Prison Complex in Melaka using a drone. The department said the suspicious activity was detected through security monitoring and control measures carried out by prison authorities before a police report was lodged for further enforcement action. The Prisons Department thanked the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) for its swift action and strategic cooperation in tackling the attempted crime.
